description:
You are assisting a data-analytics team that stores several CSV datasets in one directory on a shared Linux workstation.  
Management would like a concise, plain-text summary of the exact on-disk sizes (in **bytes**) of every CSV file in that directory, plus the total size.

Directory layout that already exists when you start:
• /home/user/data/csv/  
  ├── customers.csv  
  ├── orders.csv  
  └── returns.csv  

Requirements you must satisfy:

1. Create the directory /home/user/analysis/ if it does not yet exist.
2. Inside that directory create a new file named csv_disk_usage.log.
3. The log file must contain four lines, each terminated by a single UNIX newline (\n).  
   Format of every line (including the TOTAL line):  
   <filename>,<size_in_bytes>  
   – No spaces before or after the comma.  
   – <filename> is only the base name, **without** any leading path.  
   – <size_in_bytes> is the exact integer number of bytes reported by the filesystem for that file.  
   – Lines must appear in this exact order (alphabetically by filename, then the TOTAL line):  
     1. customers.csv,…  
     2. orders.csv,…  
     3. returns.csv,…  
     4. TOTAL,…

4. The TOTAL line’s size value must equal the arithmetic sum of the sizes reported for the three individual files.
5. Use only standard command-line utilities that are available on a typical Debian/Ubuntu system.
6. Do not modify the existing CSV files; only read them to obtain their sizes.
7. After you finish, /home/user/analysis/csv_disk_usage.log should be the *only* new file created by your work.

The whole task can be accomplished with a very small set of shell commands (around two or three), but use whatever approach you prefer as long as the result exactly matches the required output format described above.
truth:
Pre-existing files and their exact byte sizes:
• /home/user/data/csv/customers.csv  – 1250 bytes
• /home/user/data/csv/orders.csv     – 2750 bytes
• /home/user/data/csv/returns.csv    –  500 bytes

Expected content of /home/user/analysis/csv_disk_usage.log after successful completion (each line ends with a single \n):

customers.csv,1250
orders.csv,2750
returns.csv,500
TOTAL,4500

No other files or directories should be created or altered.
